jokes.text: Nothing. I just bought myself a car.
jokes.setup: Chalmers sees Skinner panting and asks what's wrong, expecting some crisis
jokes.punchline: Skinner's breathless excitement is just from buying a car
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on Skinner's usual relationship with authority and his pathetic nature

jokes.text: This will sound crazy, but at first... I thought I'd enraged you again.
jokes.setup: Skinner's nervous reaction to Chalmers
jokes.punchline: Revealing his paranoid expectation that he's constantly disappointing his boss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - his constant anxiety about disappointing authority

jokes.text: I used to think a car was just a way of getting... from point 'A' to point 'B'- and on weekends point 'C.'
jokes.setup: Standard saying about cars being transportation from A to B
jokes.punchline: Skinner's pathetically literal addition of weekend point C
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific twist on familiar phrase that reveals Skinner's rigid, limited lifestyle

jokes.text: I've always admired car owners. And I hope to be one myself as soon as I finish paying off Mother.
jokes.setup: Skinner admires car owners and wants to be one
jokes.punchline: He has to finish paying off his mother first
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Skinner's pathetic financial situation with perfect character specificity

jokes.text: She insists I pay her retroactively for the food I ate as a child.
jokes.setup: Building on the concept of paying off his mother
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail that she's charging him for childhood food retroactively
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific detail that perfectly captures the toxic Skinner/Mother dynamic

jokes.text: Holy jumping Caesar's catfish!
jokes.setup: Chalmers discovers his H has been stolen
jokes.punchline: His characteristically pretentious and elaborate exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Chalmers over-the-top vocabulary that fits his pompous character

jokes.text: My 'H' has been stolen! Oh! That's how people know it's a Honda!
jokes.setup: Discovery that the H is missing from his Honda
jokes.punchline: His panicked realization about the brand recognition importance
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about brand identity and status symbols

jokes.text: What's the point of having a Honda if you can't show it off?
jokes.setup: Continuing his panic about the missing H
jokes.punchline: Treating a Honda like it's a luxury car worth showing off
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of middle-class pretension and status anxiety over modest possessions

jokes.text: You've clearly taken items from the candy rack and placed them... on top of the doughnut in an attempt to pass them off as sprinkles.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get a doughnut with sprinkles
jokes.punchline: Apu's detailed accusation of Homer's candy-as-sprinkles scheme
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for both - Homer's cheap scheming and Apu's meticulous observation

jokes.text: A Mounds bar is not a sprinkle. A Twizzler is not a sprinkle. A Jolly Rancher is not a sprinkle, sir.
jokes.setup: Apu's accusation about fake sprinkles
jokes.punchline: His methodical listing of each inappropriate candy
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Apu's precise, formal delivery contrasting with the absurd situation

jokes.text: Perhaps in Shangri-la they are, but not here.
jokes.setup: Continuing his sprinkle lecture
jokes.punchline: Apu's philosophical reference to Shangri-la for candy sprinkles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu line - elevated language applied to mundane convenience store rules

jokes.text: Frankly, I would have expected better from Jimmy the Scumbag.
jokes.setup: Discussing the arrested criminal
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's sincere disappointment in someone literally named 'the Scumbag'
jokes.explanation: Great Wiggum obliviousness - having professional expectations of someone called 'the Scumbag'

jokes.text: Bread and water, icy showers, guards whomping your ass around the clock. And the only way out is suicide.
jokes.setup: Describing prison conditions to Homer
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact delivery ending with suicide as the escape route
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation with Wiggum's casual delivery of increasingly grim details

jokes.text: Well, I'll tell you something that's not so funny. Right now Superintendent Chalmers... is at home crying like a little girl.
jokes.setup: Students laughing at the stolen H prank
jokes.punchline: Skinner's overly dramatic description of Chalmers' emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's protective relationship with Chalmers

jokes.text: [Chuckles] I guess it is a little funny.
jokes.setup: Skinner's stern lecture about the prank not being funny
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself and admitting it's funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect character flip showing Skinner's inability to maintain authority when something genuinely amuses him

jokes.text: Balderdash. I'll just stick my head right in and- [Shouts]
jokes.setup: Skinner dismissing warnings about looking in a locker
jokes.punchline: Getting hit by eggs when he opens it
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick setup with Skinner's characteristic overconfidence before a fall

jokes.text: Half a dozen eggs? [Grunts] Well, that would be the complete dozen.
jokes.setup: Skinner covered in eggs from the prank
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact mathematical observation while egg-covered
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner response - maintaining pedantic precision even while being pranked

jokes.text: Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Nelson's locker being opened revealing stolen goods
jokes.punchline: His signature laugh even when caught red-handed
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson moment - his catchphrase perfectly timed for maximum inappropriateness

jokes.text: Damn! Dang! Darn!
jokes.setup: Nelson getting caught with stolen goods
jokes.punchline: His escalating but still mild profanity attempts
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Nelson's tough image and his inability to actually curse

jokes.text: I don't know, but he's got lethal tuna breath.
jokes.setup: Skinner asking who's 'ha-ha'-ing now
jokes.punchline: Nelson's deflecting insult about tuna breath
jokes.explanation: Good Nelson comeback that maintains his attitude even when caught

jokes.text: [In Scottish Accent] Ahh, gee. I'm standing right here, sir.
jokes.setup: Skinner describing janitorial work as the most degrading work known to man
jokes.punchline: Willie pointing out he's right there listening
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward moment with Willie's hurt reaction to being insulted unintentionally

jokes.text: Bite me. Cram it. You're dead. Get bent, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Being told to apologize to his victims
jokes.punchline: Nelson's rapid-fire defiant responses instead of apologies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Nelson's complete inability to show remorse

jokes.text: Don't you realize your butt sticks out? - It does not! - Hey! - Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Nelson insulting Lisa's posture
jokes.punchline: Lisa's indignant denial followed by apparent physical proof and Nelson's laugh
jokes.explanation: Standard schoolyard humor with Nelson getting the upper hand through crude observation

jokes.text: [Homer's Voice] Greetings, friend. Do you wish to look as happy as me?
jokes.setup: Homer's telemarketing scam message
jokes.punchline: The vague, pseudo-spiritual sales pitch for happiness
jokes.explanation: Good satire of telemarketing scams and self-help culture with Homer's earnest delivery

jokes.text: Hmm. One dollar for eternal happiness. Mmm, I'd be happier with the dollar.
jokes.setup: Homer's offer of eternal happiness for one dollar
jokes.punchline: Burns' calculating response preferring to keep the dollar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line that captures his miserly nature and twisted priorities in one economical joke

jokes.text: It's nothing but panhandling. Tele-panhandling.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizing Homer's telemarketing scheme
jokes.punchline: Her creation of the compound word 'tele-panhandling'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that accurately describes Homer's scheme while maintaining Marge's moral perspective

jokes.text: The moose? - The hoose! The hoose! - Is this right? - [Screams] Turn off the noozle! - The noodles? What noodles? - The noozle at the end of the hoose!
jokes.setup: Willie trying to teach Nelson with his thick accent
jokes.punchline: Nelson's complete misunderstanding of Willie's pronunciation leading to chaos
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating wordplay based on Willie's accent creating genuine miscommunication chaos

jokes.text: Miss Simpson... do you find something funny about the word 'tromboner'?
jokes.setup: Lisa laughing during music class
jokes.punchline: The teacher's innocent use of 'tromboner' which sounds like an innuendo
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons double entendre that works on multiple levels for different audiences

jokes.text: She was looking at Nelson! Lisa likes Nelson! - She does not! - Milhouse likes Lisa! - He does not! - Janie likes Milhouse! - She does not! - Uter likes Milhouse! - Nobody likes Milhouse!
jokes.setup: Students playing the 'who likes who' game
jokes.punchline: The chain ending with the brutal truth that nobody likes Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that builds to the devastating punchline about Milhouse's unpopularity

jokes.text: Oh. How does Bart do this every week?
jokes.setup: Lisa in detention for the first time
jokes.punchline: Her wondering how Bart handles regular detention
jokes.explanation: Good character moment highlighting the contrast between Lisa and Bart's school experiences

jokes.text: He's like a riddle wrapped in an enigma wrapped in a vest.
jokes.setup: Lisa thinking about Nelson's complexity
jokes.punchline: Adding 'wrapped in a vest' to the classic Churchill quote
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of a famous quote that perfectly captures Nelson's visual and character mystery

jokes.text: Sure is ugly though.
jokes.setup: Lisa's romantic thoughts about Nelson
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact acknowledgment of his appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic idealization with Lisa's characteristic honesty

jokes.text: Hey, Willie, catch the football! - [Bees Buzzing] - All right, I'll- [Screams]
jokes.setup: Students throwing Willie a 'football'
jokes.punchline: It's actually a beehive, causing Willie to get stung
jokes.explanation: Standard prank humor with misdirection, though fairly predictable once set up

jokes.text: I guess you could say I want to bring out the Milhouse in Nelson.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her attraction to Nelson
jokes.punchline: Using Milhouse as the ideal of sensitivity to aspire to
jokes.explanation: Clever line that uses Milhouse as the metric for sensitivity while highlighting the irony

jokes.text: But I'm all Milhouse!
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to bring out someone's inner Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse pointing out he's already entirely himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse response that highlights his desperation and the irony of his situation

jokes.text: You're more like a big sister. - No, I'm not! Why does everybody keep saying that?
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to let Milhouse down easy
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's frustrated reaction revealing this is a pattern
jokes.explanation: Good character beat that reveals Milhouse consistently gets this rejection

jokes.text: You? Why would you like me? No girls like me!
jokes.setup: Lisa revealing she sent the note
jokes.punchline: Nelson's genuine surprise and insecurity
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment showing Nelson's vulnerability beneath his tough exterior

jokes.text: Okay. But if anybody sees us, I'm just there to steal your bike.
jokes.setup: Lisa inviting Nelson over
jokes.punchline: Nelson's face-saving excuse for visiting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line balancing his need to maintain image while showing interest

jokes.text: Mmm, a little.
jokes.setup: Asked if his happiness scam makes him feel happier
jokes.punchline: Homer's minimal admission that it might work slightly
jokes.explanation: Subtle Homer moment where he inadvertently validates his own scam

jokes.text: You don't understand. She loves to climb in here. - [Shrieks] - I believe you! I don't care!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to demonstrate her cat's trick to Nelson
jokes.punchline: The cat's violent reaction and Nelson's panicked acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the gap between Lisa's gentle world and Nelson's practical fear

jokes.text: Are you nuts? I'll probably never say this to you again, but you can do better.
jokes.setup: Bart discovering Lisa likes Nelson
jokes.punchline: His rare compliment to Lisa while criticizing her choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart moment where his protective instinct overrides his usual sister rivalry

jokes.text: Milhouse likes Vaseline on toast.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Milhouse's romantic prospects
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely unrelated gross fact about Milhouse's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and disgusting detail that perfectly captures Bart's non sequitur style

jokes.text: Why, it's the AT-5000 Auto-Dialer, my very first patent.
jokes.setup: Homer's telemarketing machine calling Professor Frink
jokes.punchline: Frink revealing he invented the machine being used to scam him
jokes.explanation: Great ironic twist that Frink invented the machine now being misused for scams

jokes.text: Ah, would you listen to the gibberish they've got you saying?
jokes.setup: Frink recognizing his invention being misused
jokes.punchline: Criticizing Homer's message as gibberish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Frink showing his protective attitude toward his inventions.

jokes.text: 'Nuke The Whales'? You don't really believe that, do you? - I don't know. Gotta nuke something.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Nelson's offensive t-shirt
jokes.punchline: Nelson's casual, illogical justification for nuclear violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson logic - dismissively accepting violence as necessary without any real reasoning

jokes.text: Touché.
jokes.setup: Nelson's ridiculous nuclear philosophy
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated response to his crude logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response showing how she tries to find intellectual value in Nelson's nonsense

jokes.text: I wish I could laugh at the idea of a teacher being decapitated.
jokes.setup: Nelson's violent song about school
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual inability to enjoy violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response showing her moral struggle with Nelson's worldview. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Guts. And black stuff. And about 50 Slim Jims.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Nelson what he feels inside emotionally
jokes.punchline: Nelson's literal interpretation listing stomach contents
jokes.explanation: Excellent Nelson characterization showing his complete inability to engage with emotional concepts

jokes.text: This ought to shut her up.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment building
jokes.punchline: Nelson's unromantic motivation for kissing
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Nelson's crude approach to romance. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: You kissed a girl! - That is so gay!
jokes.setup: Nelson's gang seeing him kiss Lisa
jokes.punchline: Their illogical reaction that kissing girls is 'gay'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of juvenile male logic where showing any affection is somehow unmanly

jokes.text: He's changed, and he doesn't want to hang around with a bunch of crumb-bums.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Nelson to his gang
jokes.punchline: Her use of the antiquated insult 'crumb-bums'
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa characterization using old-fashioned language that reveals her sheltered upbringing

jokes.text: Crumb-bums? Nobody calls me a crumb-bum!
jokes.setup: Lisa calling Nelson's gang crumb-bums
jokes.punchline: Jimbo's indignant reaction to the mild insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how even mild insults can trigger disproportionate reactions

jokes.text: You're broadcasting geek rays over the entire valley.
jokes.setup: Criticism of Nelson's changed behavior
jokes.punchline: Pseudoscientific description of nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist insult with good scientific-sounding language. Memorable phrase.

jokes.text: Charlatan!
jokes.setup: Gang calling Nelson names
jokes.punchline: One of them using the sophisticated insult 'charlatan'
jokes.explanation: Excellent unexpected vocabulary from a juvenile delinquent character

jokes.text: 'Cause your mom had a three-month waiting list.
jokes.setup: Question about why Nelson is wasting time with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Nelson's crude comeback about the questioner's mother
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson comeback showing he's back to his old self with crude humor

jokes.text: Oh, brandishing your buttocks is only getting me angrier.
jokes.setup: Students mooning Skinner during the prank
jokes.punchline: Skinner's overly formal description of being mooned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner line using formal language for crude behavior, maintaining his dignity

jokes.text: Thanks for helping me out. You're a stand-up babe.
jokes.setup: Nelson thanking Lisa for hiding him
jokes.punchline: His outdated slang compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Nelson characterization with his attempt at being smooth using dated language

jokes.text: Mmm-mmm. Maybe 'cause you were the first person... that ever thought there was a nice guy inside me.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why Nelson wanted to be with her
jokes.punchline: Nelson's rare moment of genuine emotional honesty
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Nelson's capacity for self-reflection and vulnerability

jokes.text: Let's just call it 'smell you later.'
jokes.setup: Nelson asking if this is goodbye
jokes.punchline: Lisa adopting Nelson's crude farewell phrase
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback showing how Lisa has been influenced by Nelson's language

jokes.text: Yes!
jokes.setup: Lisa saying her next crush could be almost anybody
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's premature celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse ending - completely misreading the situation with characteristic optimism.

jokes.text: If you can find it in your heart to forgive me... send one dollar to Sorry Dude, 742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield.
jokes.setup: Homer's court-ordered apology calls
jokes.punchline: Turning his apology into another money-making scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ending - learning nothing and immediately starting a new variation of his scam
